content.full_text:
Dear SIR,

I AM A REAL PROPERTY APPRAISER WITH THE ASSESSORS OFFICE
IN ORANGE COUNTY. IN AN EFFORT TO AVOID ANY CONFLICTS OF INTEREST
I WOULD APPRECIATE IF YOU COULD ADVISE ME ON THE FOLLOWING
QUESTIONS WHICH ARE PREDICATED ON THE ASSUMPTION THAT DEALING IN
THE PURCHASE AND SELLING OF TRUST DEEDS SECURED BY PROPERTY IN
ORANGE COUNTY IS A CONFLICT OF INTEREST. IF THAT IS NOT THE CASE
PLEASE ADVISE.

1. IF I CONDUCT A BUSINESS IN MY SPARE TIME IN ORANGE
COUNTY, THAT BUYS AND SELLS TRUST DEEDS SECURED BY PROPERTY
OUTSIDE OF ORANGE COUNTY, IS THAT CONSIDERED A CONFLICT OF
INTEREST?

2. IF THE ABOVE QUESTION IS CONSIDERED A CONFLICT OF INTEREST, MY
2ND QUESTION IS, IF I MAINTAIN MY BROKERS LICENSE IN AN OFFICE
OUTSIDE OF ORANGE COUNTY, BUYING AND SELLING THE AFOREMENTIONED TRUST
DEEDS, WOULD THE PERIODIC USE OF MY HOME OFFICE FOR BUSINESS
PURPOSES DURING MY HOURS BE CONSIDERED A CONFLICT OF INTEREST?

3. IF I HAVE A PARTNER WHO ALSO LISTS IN ORANGE COUNTY BUT
IS NOT AN EMPLOYEE OF THE ASSESSOR OFFICE, WOULD HE BE
PRECLUDED FROM WORKING PART TIME FROM HIS HOME.

Thank you for your consideration in this matter. I will
be looking forward to hearing from you at your earliest
convenience.

Sincerely,

Don Home

[illegible stamps/markings at bottom]

sections.question:
1. If I conduct a business in my spare time in Orange County, that buys and sells trust deeds secured by property outside of Orange County, is that considered a conflict of interest?

2. If the above question is considered a conflict of interest, my 2nd question is, if I maintain my brokers license in an office outside of Orange County, buying and selling the aforementioned trust deeds, would the periodic use of my home phone for business purposes during off hours be considered a conflict of interest?

3. If I have a partner who also lives in Orange County but is not an employee of the Assessors Office, would he be precluded from working part time from his home?
sections.question_synthetic: A real property appraiser in Orange County's Assessor's Office seeks advice on whether buying and selling trust deeds (both within and outside Orange County) constitutes a conflict of interest, and whether conducting such business outside office hours from home or through an out-of-county office would mitigate any conflicts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers present. Document ID 92167 from 1992. Second page appears faint/blank in transmission.

embedding.summary: Incoming request from Dan Howe, a real property appraiser with Orange County Assessor's Office, asking whether his spare-time business activities involving purchase and sale of trust deeds create conflicts of interest, with follow-up questions about conducting business outside work hours and through external office locations.
